fields.description:
## Why

Three documentation artifacts make stale or over-broad claims that the code contradicts:

- `docs/concurrency.md` (~lines 263-265) says the detached-HEAD-local-ahead edge has no
  dedicated automated regression test — but
  `tests/unit/test_sync_union_recovery.py::test_sync_preserves_detached_head_local_ahead_commit`
  is exactly that test.
- `README.md` (~lines 146-151) still describes a util-linux/BusyBox external `flock`
  contract, while the implementation (`src/rebar/_store/lock.py`) uses a Python
  `fcntl.flock` + atomic `mkdir` dual-window lock with no external `flock` binary.
- `docs/mutation-testing.md` presents high mutation scores without scoping them to the
  five selected cores (nothing about sync/push/lock/compact/reconciler orchestration).

## What

- Correct all three docs and reclassify the structural tripwire tests in any coverage
  inventory as supplemental (not behavioral coverage).

## Scope

- `docs/concurrency.md`, `README.md`, `docs/mutation-testing.md`
- Coverage-inventory notes / docstrings for the leaf AST guard, registry-completeness
  assertion, and source-string timer scan.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `docs/concurrency.md` references the existing detached-HEAD regression test instead
      of claiming none exists.
- [ ] `README.md` describes the current Python `fcntl` + `mkdir` dual-window lock and
      drops the util-linux/BusyBox external-`flock` framing (no Windows claim).
- [ ] `docs/mutation-testing.md` qualifies its scores as "within the selected scope
      (5 cores)".
- [ ] The three named structural tripwire tests — which already exist — are labeled
      supplemental/structural in the coverage inventory (no behavioral-coverage credit):
      `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/orchestrate/test_leaves_registry_coverage.py::test_every_leaf_has_nonstub_body_structural_guard`
      (leaf AST guard),
      `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/orchestrate/test_leaves_effect_matrix.py::test_matrix_covers_every_leaf`
      (registry-completeness), and
      `tests/unit/test_llm_timeouts.py::test_no_total_runtime_timer_mechanism`
      (source-string timer scan). This is a labeling/inventory change only; the tests are
      not modified.
- [ ] `make lint` passes (docs/format checks).
